title: oxytropis viscida var. hudsonica
intro: Oxytropis viscida var. hudsonica, also known as Hudson's globetrotter or globetrotter pea, is a perennial legume native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which help to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Oxytropis viscida var. hudsonica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory and skin problems. Herbal preparations of Oxytropis viscida var. hudsonica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to skin conditions and wounds.
